meta.purpose: Golden eval set for the NL->facet query-understanding router (Atlas A1). Each case pins the ideal structured interpretation of a natural-language question so any router (today's deterministic resolve_facets, or a future LLM router) can be scored against a fixed target. Curate this by hand; it is ground truth, not generated.
meta.how_to_read: expect.* is the IDEAL router output. baseline.* is a hand-predicted grade of the CURRENT deterministic resolver (app/api/research_facets.py + research.py dimension map), confirmed by tests/eval/score_baseline.py. The gap between them is the router's job.

role_vocab.filter: hard scope -> becomes a WHERE clause (today's only behavior).
role_vocab.illustration: an EXAMPLE of a broader filter ('electronics like phones'); must NOT become an independent AND filter. This is the lever the deterministic scanner cannot pull.
role_vocab.reference: a named subject used for comparison, not a scope ('comparable to France's AGEC across all regions'). resolve_facets already does this for places via _EXPANSION_CUES; the router generalizes it.

facet_slug_sources.materials: keys of _MATERIAL_ALIASES in app/api/research_facets.py (canonical material_categories). Group 'packaging' expands to plastic_packaging,paper_packaging,glass,metals.
facet_slug_sources.dimensions: keys of _DIM_TRIGGERS in app/api/research.py: eco_modulation, recycled_content, collection_targets, pro_structure, labeling, penalties, fee_amounts, bans_restrictions.
facet_slug_sources.places: Jurisdiction.name from the DB alias table (data, not code).

scoring.slug_extraction: set precision/recall/F1 of extracted vs expected slugs per facet type (materials/instruments/products/dimensions). Places need a DB and are scored separately.
scoring.role_accuracy: fraction of expected facets whose ROLE (filter/illustration/reference) the router got right. Deterministic baseline = every extracted facet is implicitly 'filter', so non-filter expectations count as role errors.
scoring.intent_accuracy: exact-match on intent. Deterministic baseline has no intent field -> 0 by construction.
scoring.headline: role_accuracy + intent_accuracy on the illustrative-vs-filter, reference, and count categories is the number that must move when the router ships.

cases.question: Is there any examples from France to compare to the US?
cases.why: Section 2a failure #1. France must be a jurisdiction filter (not a body-text token). Guards the facet layer; the original miss was downstream ranking/narration.

baseline.note: Places resolve (France via alias, US via uppercase-code). intent=compare not captured.

cases.question: records about the france anti waste law? AGEC?
cases.why: Section 2a failure #2. AGEC was buried at rank 346 by OR-broad noise. Facet layer must set France=filter and keep 'agec'/'anti waste' as free_text; ranking is the A1.2 fix.

baseline.note: France->filter, agec survives to free_text. rank-346 dilution is downstream of routing.

cases.question: comparable laws to France's AGEC across all regions
cases.why: France is a REFERENCE subject, not a scope. 'across all regions' + 'comparable' must NOT lock retrieval to France.

baseline.note: _EXPANSION_CUES already yields reference_labels=[France] (role=reference correct!); intent=compare still not captured.

cases.question: how does California's approach compare to other states?
cases.why: California is the reference subject; 'other states' is an expansion cue -> don't scope to CA.

baseline.note: 'other states' is an _EXPANSION_CUE -> reference. intent=compare missed.

cases.question: are there any similar examples to the French repair index in the corpus?
cases.why: France's repair index (AGEC indice de réparabilité) is the exemplar; the user wants comparable measures across the whole corpus. Needs (1) France=reference not scope, (2) intent=compare, (3) a repairability DIMENSION to target the repair-scoring/index/label subset — NARROWER than the broad right_to_repair instrument (378 bills). Surfaces the missing repairability dimension; the instrument alone can't isolate index/scoring measures.

baseline.note: No repairability dimension exists (not a compliance_details envelope, not a _DIM_TRIGGER); right_to_repair is only the broad instrument. Deterministic resolver has no such concept and no intent. This case defines the target for the new repairability dimension.

cases.question: What are the most unique types of bills and laws across the corpus for example material specific incentives like texas's oyster bills, france's repairability index, etc?
cases.why: Prod regression (2026-07-15). A corpus-DIVERSITY ask ('most unique TYPES across the corpus') whose named entities are all EXAMPLES: Texas/France are benchmarks (reference, via the 'across the corpus' everywhere-cue), and 'incentives'/'repairability index' merely illustrate what a 'unique type' means. The deterministic path collapsed the answer to dimension:repairability (389 repair bills): the multi-term free-text AND found 0, and the RULE-2 fallback latched onto the lone word 'repairability' sitting inside the France example. Ideal interpretation: NO hard filter at all — least of all a repairability dimension. Fix lives at the retrieval layer (an illustrative-aside guard on RULE 2 + a diversity/outlier sampler, RULE 2.5, that spreads across instrument_type clusters); this case guards that the facet interpretation derives no spurious scope so retrieval is free to sample the corpus's variety.

baseline.note: Post-fix: France+Texas resolve as reference (everywhere-cue), no material/instrument/product scope, and dimensions is empty because _trigger_is_illustrative suppresses the illustrative 'repairability' — so the facet layer now derives the correct no-scope interpretation. The diversity SAMPLING itself (RULE 2.5 in _relevant_bills) is a retrieval mode not representable in this facet schema; it is downstream of what this case scores. intent=list still missing (no field).

cases.question: reuse and refill targets for packaging
cases.why: Defines the reuse_refill dimension target — the biggest keyword-theme orphan (125 hits). Reuse/refill mandates are a compliance requirement, not a material or instrument.

baseline.note: After the 2026-07-13 trigger add, _map_dimension routes reuse_refill; retrieval degrades (no envelope yet). Intent absent in deterministic.

cases.question: which bills require a digital product passport for batteries?
cases.why: Defines the digital_product_passport dimension target (EU ESPR / battery reg). Distinct from generic labeling — it's a traceability/disclosure requirement.

baseline.note: batteries resolves; DPP dimension routes after the trigger add but has no envelope yet.

cases.question: refurbishment and remanufacturing standards for electronics
cases.why: Defines the remanufacturing dimension target (25 hits). Refurbishment/remanufacturing standards as a requirement facet.

baseline.note: electronics resolves; remanufacturing dimension routes after the trigger add but has no envelope yet.

cases.question: EPR bills on electronics like phones and laptops
cases.why: THE canonical lever. 'phones'/'laptops' are illustrations of the electronics filter, not independent product filters. This is what deterministic scanning cannot decide.

baseline.note: Extracts epr+electronics+phones+laptops all as filters. _scope_extra ANDs material=electronics AND product IN (phones,laptops); an electronics EPR bill without per-product coverage rows for BOTH phones and laptops is wrongly excluded.

cases.question: which bills cover laptops?
cases.why: Discriminator partner to the above. Same token 'laptops' but here it IS the filter. A router must not over-generalize illustration detection.

baseline.note: product=laptops as filter is correct here. Deterministic gets this one right; the router must too.

cases.question: recycled content rules for packaging such as bottles and cans
cases.why: 'bottles and cans' illustrate packaging; should not become hard product/material filters on top of the packaging group.

baseline.note: 'packaging' group expands correctly; 'bottles'/'cans' fall to free_text (no standalone alias). Dimension recycled_content is resolved in research.py, not the facet resolver -> split ownership.

cases.question: textile take-back schemes covering clothing and footwear
cases.why: clothing/footwear illustrate the textiles filter; ANDing them as product filters over-narrows.

baseline.note: textiles + clothing + footwear all extracted as filters -> _scope_extra ANDs product coverage IN (clothing,footwear), over-filtering the textiles set.

cases.question: what's happening with e-waste in the EU?
cases.why: Colloquial 'e-waste' + region code. Shows a paraphrase the alias table DOES cover (electronics) and a jurisdiction subtree (EU).

baseline.note: 'e-waste' aliased to electronics; 'EU' uppercase -> jurisdiction. Works today.

cases.question: any rules about throwing away old mattresses?
cases.why: Indirect phrasing for a material. Material alias fires; the router should also strip 'throwing away old' chrome that survives today.

baseline.note: mattresses matched; 'throwing/away/old' pollute free_text (not stopwords).

cases.question: single-use plastic bans
cases.why: Compound material alias + dimension trigger.

baseline.note: 'single-use plastic' -> plastic_packaging; 'bans' -> bans_restrictions (research.py).

cases.question: smartphone take-back and repairability requirements
cases.why: ALIAS-COVERAGE GAP: 'repairability' is not in _INSTRUMENT_ALIASES ('right to repair' only), so the scanner misses the right_to_repair instrument. The router should infer it.

baseline.note: 'smartphone'->phones works; 'repairability' does NOT match right_to_repair (alias is 'right to repair'). Instrument missed entirely.

cases.question: cigarette butt litter laws
cases.why: Out-of-taxonomy subject. Correct behavior is NO facet + everything to free_text. Tests that the ROUTER also refuses to hallucinate a slug.

baseline.note: No facet aliases fire; all to free_text. Correct. The risk here is a future LLM router inventing a bogus material.

cases.question: PFAS restrictions in food packaging
cases.why: PFAS is a substance not in the material taxonomy -> free_text; 'packaging' -> group filter; 'restrictions' -> dimension.

baseline.note: 'packaging' group fires; PFAS->free_text (correct); 'restrict' dimension in research.py. Arguably packaging is too broad for 'food packaging' - router could narrow to plastic+paper.

cases.question: Georgia recycling laws
cases.why: 'Georgia' = US state OR country. Router should surface BOTH (spec open decision), not silently pick one.

baseline.note: alias 'georgia' matches both nodes -> both in place_ids, but no ambiguity flag surfaced to the user. Needs DB to confirm.

cases.question: how many US states have battery EPR?
cases.why: Intent=count changes the answer shape (a number, not a list). Nothing today captures count-vs-list.

baseline.note: batteries+epr+US all resolve as filters. intent=count entirely absent -> answer would list, not count.

cases.question: compare recycled-content mandates between California and the EU
cases.why: Two-jurisdiction compare + dimension. intent=compare should drive a side-by-side answer.

baseline.note: CA + EU resolve; 'recycled-content' dimension. intent=compare missed.

cases.question: most compelling incentives
cases.why: Known good case (spec A1.5 #3). Must still resolve to the eco_modulation set, not a junk text search.

baseline.note: Dimension routing ('incentiv'->eco_modulation) lives in _relevant_bills and competes with the free-text tier; run the harness/prod to confirm which wins.

cases.question: deposit return for beverage containers
cases.why: Known good precise case. Instrument alias must win.

baseline.note: 'deposit return' -> deposit_return instrument. 'beverage containers' to free_text.

cases.question: paint EPR in the US
cases.why: Clean multi-facet regression guard (material + instrument + place).

baseline.note: paint+epr+US all resolve. Clean today.

cases.question: carpet stewardship programs
cases.why: Material + PRO-structure dimension; 'stewardship programs' should ideally hit pro_structure.

baseline.note: carpet matched; but _DIM_TRIGGERS wants 'stewardship organization/plan', so bare 'stewardship programs' likely misses pro_structure -> a dimension gap the router can close.

cases.question: packaging EPR everywhere except California
cases.why: STRETCH. Exclusion is not in the Facets contract. Tracks whether the router (or a later phase) can express NOT-a-place.

baseline.note: California would be ADDED as a filter (opposite of intent). Exclusion unsupported end to end; expected-fail until a later phase. Included to track the ceiling.

cases.question: what about just California?
cases.why: STRETCH (A2 contextual rewrite). Standalone this is meaningless; with prior turn 'tires EPR' it means 'tires EPR in California'. Defines the follow-up target.

baseline.note: Stateless resolver sees only 'what about just California?' -> place=California, loses tires+epr. Needs the A2 contextual rewrite; excluded from the offline baseline run.
